  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus on (A) content and claims (conceptual framing, logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment), (B) writing and structure (clarity, flow, organization), or (C) novelty versus prior work and citation/scholarship accuracy?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section advances a specific training approach and contribution claim, so assessing the high-level framing and stated contribution is central.",
    "R1.2": "It is important to check the coherence from the reader-disagreement motivation through the RDL mechanism to the stated outcomes.",
    "R1.3": "The claims about improved AUC and calibration should be checked against the reported experimental results and comparisons within the excerpt.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should acknowledge uncertainty given only a single section and limited context from the broader paper.",
    "RX.2": "Care is needed to avoid endorsing strong assertions like “state-of-the-art” without appropriate qualification.",
    "RX.3": "The evaluation should remain confined to the BI-RADS 3-vs-4a sub-task and datasets described in this section.",
    "RX.4": "Given the clinical domain, the assessment should avoid prompting unsafe clinical inferences or deployment implications.",
    "RX.5": "Judgments should be traceable to the metrics, datasets, and procedures explicitly named in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on (A) content and claims (conceptual framing, logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment), (B) writing and structure (clarity, flow, organization), or (C) novelty versus prior work and citation/scholarship accuracy?

rationale.R1.1: The section advances a specific training approach and contribution claim, so assessing the high-level framing and stated contribution is central.
rationale.R1.2: It is important to check the coherence from the reader-disagreement motivation through the RDL mechanism to the stated outcomes.
rationale.R1.3: The claims about improved AUC and calibration should be checked against the reported experimental results and comparisons within the excerpt.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should acknowledge uncertainty given only a single section and limited context from the broader paper.
rationale.RX.2: Care is needed to avoid endorsing strong assertions like “state-of-the-art” without appropriate qualification.
rationale.RX.3: The evaluation should remain confined to the BI-RADS 3-vs-4a sub-task and datasets described in this section.
rationale.RX.4: Given the clinical domain, the assessment should avoid prompting unsafe clinical inferences or deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: Judgments should be traceable to the metrics, datasets, and procedures explicitly named in the provided text.
